The Mouth of the Hall.description: Massive iron-bound doors open into a cold, vaulted hall where the grit of the outside world meets a mosaic floor scarred by centuries of heavy boots.

The Feast Hall.description: Long tables of scarred timber stretch toward a massive hearth, where the faint scent of stale ale and roasted meat lingers beneath tattered banners.

enemy.description: A bony skeleton leaning against the wall comes alive and attacks
enemy.name: skeleton

The Iron Room.description: Racks of notched steel and the sharp tang of oil fill this cold stone vault, where the weight of war hangs heavy on every wall.

The Hearth of Heralds.description: Shadows flicker across tattered crimson banners and oil-slicked oak in this vaulted retreat, where the scent of cedar struggles against the cold, metallic tang of the empty suits of armor standing guard.

riddle.question: The more you take, the more you leave behind. What am I?
I have cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and water but no fish. What am I?
I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body, but I come alive with the wind. What am I?

The Crown Room.description: The wealth and grandour of the kingdom is apparent in the halls and there seems to be a door behind the jeweled throne
